generatedSentence: A brilliant female hacker, unassailable in her pursuit of originality, sat at her desk, her dachshund napping near an antique inkwell and a single lotus. The airflow through her window carried a faint accented undertone from a distant trucker, a stark contrast to the tableau of her screens displaying the current, struggling economy. She was not enamored with the corporate systems dominating the world, recognizing the subtle but pervasive danger they posed. Her latest hypothesis involved a unique gadget, a unicum solution, for a complex journey of planification. This was no gluttonous undertaking, but a critical note in her long-term mission. Suddenly, an outage plunged her workspace into darkness, the electrical grid fizzled, and the silence was broken only by the chirping crickets outside her windows. This was a clear ultimatum, a wicked warning detected by her advanced gadgets: a torrent of attacks from hackers who sought to nullify her efforts. She knew a polymorph virus was at play, an abstruse infection designed to make her systems misbehave. The horror was palpable, a thunder of impending doom, as the internals of her network were being rapidly compromised. She grabbed her morningstar device, realizing the gameplay had just turned deadly. With the intensity of fifty lumberjacks chopping through an apple orchard, the hacker launched her counter-attack, ignoring the boredom of standard protocols. She watched as her jetpack-wearing kid-protégé, a geriatric walker by day but a cyber-warrior by night, races through virtual karts and kayaks to combat the digital threat. The enemy, an overpowered digital drunkard fueled by tobacco and sophistry, tried to violate their firewall, but her harem of defensive bots held the line. She knew this was the climax, a desperate fight to prevent their efforts from being nailed to the wall, a battle against the uglier junk of the digital abyss. As the dust settled, an epiphany struck, a firefly of understanding illuminating the bracket of her screen. She saw the triumph, a wonderful memento of their victory, as the last vestiges of the attack were jettisoned. She had proven the coverage was strong, the enemy's veal-like resistance collapsing under her might. There was an allergic reaction of joy, a nutty, fabulous celebration, as her woolly pet donkey (a metaphorical one, of course) nudged her, a delicacy of butter and chocolate awaiting. She took a moment to thank her team, knowing that even after such a roundabout journey, this serene scenery of the hangar-like server room signaled the end of their virtual vacations, ready for the next challenge.
panelSentences: A brilliant female hacker, unassailable in her pursuit of originality, sat at her desk, her dachshund napping near an antique inkwell and a single lotus. The airflow through her window carried a faint accented undertone from a distant trucker, a stark contrast to the tableau of her screens displaying the current, struggling economy. She was not enamored with the corporate systems dominating the world, recognizing the subtle but pervasive danger they posed. Her latest hypothesis involved a unique gadget, a unicum solution, for a complex journey of planification. This was no gluttonous undertaking, but a critical note in her long-term mission.

Suddenly, an outage plunged her workspace into darkness, the electrical grid fizzled, and the silence was broken only by the chirping crickets outside her windows. This was a clear ultimatum, a wicked warning detected by her advanced gadgets: a torrent of attacks from hackers who sought to nullify her efforts. She knew a polymorph virus was at play, an abstruse infection designed to make her systems misbehave. The horror was palpable, a thunder of impending doom, as the internals of her network were being rapidly compromised. She grabbed her morningstar device, realizing the gameplay had just turned deadly.

With the intensity of fifty lumberjacks chopping through an apple orchard, the hacker launched her counter-attack, ignoring the boredom of standard protocols. She watched as her jetpack-wearing kid-protégé, a geriatric walker by day but a cyber-warrior by night, races through virtual karts and kayaks to combat the digital threat. The enemy, an overpowered digital drunkard fueled by tobacco and sophistry, tried to violate their firewall, but her harem of defensive bots held the line. She knew this was the climax, a desperate fight to prevent their efforts from being nailed to the wall, a battle against the uglier junk of the digital abyss.

As the dust settled, an epiphany struck, a firefly of understanding illuminating the bracket of her screen. She saw the triumph, a wonderful memento of their victory, as the last vestiges of the attack were jettisoned. She had proven the coverage was strong, the enemy's veal-like resistance collapsing under her might. There was an allergic reaction of joy, a nutty, fabulous celebration, as her woolly pet donkey (a metaphorical one, of course) nudged her, a delicacy of butter and chocolate awaiting. She took a moment to thank her team, knowing that even after such a roundabout journey, this serene scenery of the hangar-like server room signaled the end of their virtual vacations, ready for the next challenge.
rawPanelSentences: A brilliant **female** hacker, unassailable in her pursuit of **originality**, sat at her desk, her **dachshund** napping near an antique **inkwell** and a single **lotus**. The **airflow** through her window carried a faint **accented undertone** from a distant **trucker**, a stark contrast to the **tableau** of her screens displaying the current, struggling **economy**. She was not **enamored** with the corporate **systems** dominating the world, recognizing the subtle but pervasive **danger** they posed. Her latest **hypothesis** involved a unique **gadget**, a **unicum** solution, for a complex **journey** of **planification**. This was no **gluttonous** undertaking, but a critical **note** in her long-term mission.

Suddenly, an **outage** plunged her workspace into darkness, the **electrical** grid **fizzled**, and the silence was broken only by the chirping **crickets** outside her **windows**. This was a clear **ultimatum**, a wicked warning **detected** by her advanced **gadgets**: a **torrent** of **attacks** from **hackers** who sought to **nullify** her efforts. She knew a **polymorph** virus was at play, an **abstruse infection** designed to make her **systems misbehave**. The **horror** was palpable, a **thunder** of impending doom, as the **internals** of her network were being rapidly compromised. She grabbed her **morningstar** device, realizing the **gameplay** had just turned deadly.

With the **intensity** of **fifty lumberjacks** chopping through an **apple** orchard, the hacker launched her counter-attack, ignoring the **boredom** of standard protocols. She watched as her **jetpack**-wearing **kid**-protégé, a **geriatric walker** by day but a cyber-warrior by night, **races** through virtual **karts** and **kayaks** to combat the digital threat. The enemy, an **overpowered** digital **drunkard** fueled by **tobacco** and **sophistry**, tried to violate their firewall, but her **harem** of defensive bots held the line. She knew this was the **climax**, a desperate fight to prevent their efforts from being **nailed** to the wall, a battle against the **uglier junk** of the digital **abyss**.

As the dust settled, an **epiphany** struck, a **firefly** of understanding illuminating the **bracket** of her screen. She saw the **triumph**, a wonderful **memento** of their victory, as the last vestiges of the attack were **jettisoned**. She had proven the **coverage** was strong, the enemy's **veal**-like resistance collapsing under her might. There was an allergic reaction of joy, a **nutty**, **fabulous** celebration, as her **woolly** pet **donkey** (a metaphorical one, of course) nudged her, a delicacy of **butter** and **chocolate** awaiting. She took a moment to **thank** her team, knowing that even after such a **roundabout** journey, this serene **scenery** of the **hangar**-like server room signaled the end of their virtual **vacations**, ready for the next challenge.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, cyberpunk synthwave style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a female hacker with a glowing blue data stream tattoo on her arm, wearing sleek black armored overalls with neon green accents and a visor that projects code. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist intensely typing on a holographic keyboard, a small dachshund curled at her feet, close-up, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Power grid sparking and failing, a shadowy figure's hand reaching through a digital portal, wide shot, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist in mid-air with a subtle jetpack glow, wielding a data-mace against an abstract, glitching digital monster, dynamic action shot, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist standing victorious amidst calm, glowing servers, looking out a large window at a serene digital landscape, medium shot, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting.

definitions.definition: To rest on one's bent knees, sometimes only one; to move to such a position.
To cause to kneel.
definitions.example: She knelt the doll to fit it into the box.

definitions.definition: To rest on (one's) knees
definitions.example: He knelt him down to pray.

definitions.definition: Any plant of the genus Nicotiana.
Leaves of Nicotiana tabacum and some other species cultivated and harvested to make cigarettes, cigars, snuff, for smoking in pipes or for chewing.
A variety of tobacco.
definitions.example: Tobaccos from the Connecticut Valley were used for wrapping cigars.

definitions.definition: To indulge in tobacco; to smoke.
To treat with tobacco.
Exposure to likely harm; peril.
definitions.example: There's plenty of danger in the desert.

definitions.definition: An instance or cause of likely harm.
Mischief.
(mainly outside US) The stop indication of a signal (usually in the phrase "at danger").
definitions.example: The north signal was at danger because of the rockslide.

definitions.definition: Ability to harm; someone's dominion or power to harm or penalise. See in one's danger, below.
Liability.
Difficulty; sparingness.
Coyness; disdainful behavior.
To claim liability.
To imperil; to endanger.
To run the risk.
A certain breed of dog having short legs and a long trunk, including miniature, long-haired, and short-haired varieties.
Something, such as a belief, that cannot be assailed.
Secure against attack; impregnable.
(by extension) Undeniable, incontestable or incontrovertible.
definitions.example: She won the debate with her unassailable logic.

definitions.definition: A contest between people, animals, vehicles, etc. where the goal is to be the first to reach some objective. Example: Several horses run in a horse race, and the first one to reach the finishing post wins
definitions.example: The race around the park was won by Johnny, who ran faster than the others.

definitions.definition: Swift progress; rapid motion; an instance of moving or driving at high speed.
A race condition.
A progressive movement toward a goal.
definitions.example: the race to cure cancer

definitions.definition: A fast-moving current of water, such as that which powers a mill wheel.
A water channel, esp. one built to lead water to or from a point where it is utilised.
Competitive action of any kind, especially when prolonged; hence, career; course of life.
The bushings of a rolling element bearing which contacts the rolling elements.
To take part in a race (in the sense of a contest).
definitions.example: The drivers were racing around the track.

definitions.definition: To compete against in such a race.
definitions.example: I raced him to the car, but he was there first, so he got to ride shotgun.

definitions.definition: To move or drive at high speed; to hurry or speed.
definitions.example: As soon as it was time to go home, he raced for the door.

definitions.definition: Of a motor, to run rapidly when not engaged to a transmission.
A group of sentient beings, particularly people, distinguished by common ancestry, heritage or characteristics:
A population geographically separated from others of its species that develops significantly different characteristics; an informal term for a subspecies.
A breed or strain of domesticated animal.
A category or species of something that has emerged or evolved from an older one (with an implied parallel to animal breeding or evolutionary science).
definitions.example: Recent developments in artificial intelligence has brought about a new race of robots that can perform household chores without supervision.

definitions.definition: Peculiar flavour, taste, or strength, as of wine; that quality, or assemblage of qualities, which indicates origin or kind, as in wine; hence, characteristic flavour.
Characteristic quality or disposition.
To assign a race to; to perceive as having a (usually specified) race.
A rhizome or root, especially of ginger.
To demolish; to level to the ground.
To scrape as if with a razor.
Any flow of air, especially the motion of air around a moving aircraft or aerofoil.
A sum or source of money.
definitions.example: a fund for the maintenance of underprivileged students

definitions.definition: An organization managing such money.
A money-management operation, such as a mutual fund.
definitions.example: Several major funds were declared insolvent recently.

definitions.definition: A large supply of something to be drawn upon.
definitions.example: He drew on his immense fund of knowledge.

definitions.definition: To pay for.
definitions.example: He used his inheritance to fund his gambling addiction.

definitions.definition: To place (money) in a fund.
To form a debt into a stock charged with interest.
A sweater or similar garment made of wool.
A sheep not yet shorn.
A piece of woolwork.
Made of wool.
definitions.example: Put on a woolly jumper and turn down the thermostat.

definitions.definition: Having a thick, soft texture, as if made of wool.
definitions.example: There was nothing left in the fruit bowl but a brown banana and a couple of woolly pears.

definitions.definition: (of thinking, principles, etc.) Based on emotions rather than logic.
definitions.example: That's the sort of woolly thinking that causes wars to start.

definitions.definition: Unclear, fuzzy, hazy, cloudy.
Clothed in wool.
A large garage-like structure where aircraft are kept.
definitions.example: The plane taxied on over to the hangar for repairs.

definitions.definition: A covered shed for carriages.
To store (an aircraft) in a hangar.

Used loosely, a tentative conjecture explaining an observation, phenomenon or scientific problem that can be tested by further observation, investigation and/or experimentation. As a scientific term of art, see the attached quotation. Compare to theory, and quotation given there.
(general) An assumption taken to be true for the purpose of argument or investigation.
(grammar) The antecedent of a conditional statement.
Given to excessive eating; prone to overeating.
Greedy.
A coarse mediaeval coat of defence, especially one made of leather.
A set amount of travelling, seen as a single unit; a discrete trip, a voyage.
Any process or progression likened to a journey, especially one that involves difficulties or personal development.
definitions.example: my journey of dealing with grief

definitions.definition: A day.
A day's travelling; the distance travelled in a day.
A day's work.
The weight of finished coins delivered at one time to the Master of the Mint.
(collective) A group of giraffes.
To travel, to make a trip or voyage.
The act or process of infecting.
An uncontrolled growth of harmful microorganisms in a host.
A collection of organized things; a whole composed of relationships among its members.
definitions.example: There are eight planets in the solar system.

definitions.definition: A method or way of organizing or planning.
definitions.example: Followers should have a system to follow that works in their interests, not against them.

definitions.definition: A striking and vivid representation; a picture.
A vivid graphic scene of a group of people arranged as in a painting or bas relief sculpture.
Hence, an arrangement of actors in static positions on stage, having the effect of pointing up a particular moment in the drama, conventionally revealed by opening tableau curtains (known as "tabs").
A table (two-dimensional presentation of data).
Mostly in solitaire card games, but also in other card and board games, the main area, where random cards can be arranged.
A semantic tableau.
A final statement of terms or conditions made by one party to another, especially one that expresses a threat of reprisal or war.
(mostly in the passive, followed by "of" or "with") To cause to be in love.
(mostly in the passive) To captivate.
In love, amorous.
definitions.example: She's enamored of [or with] her new boyfriend.

definitions.definition: An attempt to cause damage, injury to, or death of opponent or enemy.
An attempt to detract from the worth or credibility of, a person, position, idea, object, or thing, by physical, verbal, emotional, or other assault.
definitions.example: They claimed the censorship of the article was an attack on free speech.

definitions.definition: A time in which one attacks; the offence of a battle.
definitions.example: The army timed their attack to coincide with the local celebrations.

definitions.definition: (by extension) The beginning of active operations on anything.
definitions.example: Having washed the plates from dinner, I made an attack on the laundry.

definitions.definition: An attempt to exploit a vulnerability in a computer system.
Collectively, the bowlers of a cricket side.
Any contact with the ball other than a serve or block which sends the ball across the plane of the net.
The three attackmen on the field or all the attackmen of a team.
The sudden onset of a disease or condition.
definitions.example: I've had an attack of the flu.

definitions.definition: An active episode of a chronic or recurrent disease.
The onset of a musical note, particularly with respect to the strength (and duration) of that onset.

(audio) The amount of time it takes for the volume of an audio signal to go from zero to maximum level (e.g. an audio waveform representing a snare drum hit would feature a very fast attack, whereas that of a wave washing to shore would feature a slow attack).
To apply violent force to someone or something.
definitions.example: This species of snake will only attack humans if it feels threatened.

definitions.definition: To aggressively challenge a person, idea, etc., with words (particularly in newspaper headlines, because it typesets into less space than "criticize" or similar).
definitions.example: She published an article attacking the recent pay cuts.

definitions.definition: To begin to affect; to act upon injuriously or destructively; to begin to decompose or waste.
To deal with something in a direct way; to set to work upon.
definitions.example: I attacked the meal with a hearty appetite.

definitions.definition: To aim balls at the batsman’s wicket.
To set a field, or bowl in a manner designed to get wickets.
To bat aggressively, so as to score runs quickly.
To move forward in an active attempt to score a point, as opposed to trying not to concede.
To accelerate quickly in an attempt to get ahead of the other riders.
Difficult to comprehend or understand.
Concealed or hidden out of the way; secret.
A banknote or coin with a denomination of 50.
definitions.example: Do you want small bills or are fifties OK?

definitions.definition: A batsman's score of at least 50 runs and less than 100 runs.
The cardinal number occurring after forty-nine and before fifty-one.
The agent noun of to walk: a person who walks or a thing which walks, especially a pedestrian or a participant in a walking race.
A walking frame.
(often in the plural) A shoe designed for comfortable walking.
A zombie.
A male escort who accompanies a woman to an event.
A gressorial bird.
A forester.
A kind of military robot or mecha with legs for locomotion.
An old person.
Relating to the elderly
Elderly, old
Relating to geriatrics
Any organism that shows polymorphism.
Any substance or mineral that forms different types of crystal.
The transformation of an item or creature into something different by magic.
To transform; to change into another form.
To transform into something different by magic.
A container for ink, designed and usually positioned so that a person may conveniently dip a pen into it whenever a refill is needed.
The quality of being original or novel.
The capacity to think independently or be inventive.
Something original.
Cunning, sometimes manifested as trickery.
The art of using deceptive speech or writing.
An argument that seems plausible, but is fallacious or misleading, especially one devised deliberately to be so.
The flesh of a calf (i.e. a young bovine) used for food.
A conclusive success following an effort, conflict, or confrontation of obstacles; victory; conquest.
definitions.example: the triumph of knowledge

definitions.definition: A magnificent and imposing ceremonial performed in honor of a victor.
Any triumphal procession; a pompous exhibition; a stately show or pageant.
A state of joy or exultation at success.
A trump card.
A card game, also called trump.
A ceremony held to publicly celebrate and sanctify the military achievement of an army commander.
A work of art, cuisine, etc. of very high quality.
definitions.example: Scorsese's latest film is a triumph.

definitions.definition: An expression of appreciation; a thought.
A domestic animal, Equus asinus asinus, similar to a horse
A stubborn person
A fool
A small auxiliary engine
A bad poker player
Displeasing to the eye; not aesthetically pleasing.
Displeasing to the ear or some other sense.
Offensive to one's sensibilities or morality.
definitions.example: He played an ugly trick on us.

definitions.definition: Ill-natured; crossgrained; quarrelsome.
definitions.example: an ugly temper; to feel ugly

definitions.definition: Unpleasant; disagreeable; likely to cause trouble or loss.
definitions.example: With all this competition, expect things to get ugly.

definitions.definition: A person whose work is to fell trees.
A lumberjacket.
A common, round fruit produced by the tree Malus domestica, cultivated in temperate climates.

Any of various tree-borne fruits or vegetables especially considered as resembling an apple; also (with qualifying words) used to form the names of other specific fruits such as custard apple, rose apple, thorn apple etc.
The fruit of the Tree of Knowledge, eaten by Adam and Eve according to post-Biblical Christian tradition; the forbidden fruit.
A tree of the genus Malus, especially one cultivated for its edible fruit; the apple tree.
The wood of the apple tree.
(in the plural) Short for apples and pears, slang for stairs.
The ball in baseball.
When smiling, the round, fleshy part of the cheeks between the eyes and the corners of the mouth.
A Native American or red-skinned person who acts and/or thinks like a white (Caucasian) person.
(ice hockey slang) An assist.
To become apple-like.
To form buds.
An amount by which something or someone is covered.
definitions.example: Before laying sod on that clay, the ground needs two inches of coverage with topsoil.

definitions.definition: The amount of space or time given to an event in newspapers or on television.
The average number of reads representing a given nucleotide in the reconstructed sequence.
The area covered by a mobile phone (cellphone) or other radio network.
The signal strength, reception of a radio signal.
definitions.example: Mobile phone coverage is poor in some areas.

definitions.definition: Journalism: the attention given by the press or news media about some news; the total of stories published or broadcast about a topic.
Defense.
A thing whose name cannot be remembered; thingamajig, doohickey.
Any device or machine, especially one whose name cannot be recalled. Often either clever or complicated.
definitions.example: He bought a neat new gadget for shredding potatoes.

definitions.definition: Any consumer electronics product.
A sequence of machine code instructions crafted as part of an exploit that attempts to divert execution to a memory location chosen by the attacker.
The totality of player experiences during a game, such as the gaming environment and the competitive interaction with other players; often with reference to a video game.
(collective) Items that have been or are about to be ejected from a boat or balloon.
The action of jettisoning items.
To eject from a boat, submarine, aircraft, spaceship or hot-air balloon, so as to lighten the load.
definitions.example: The ballooners had to jettison all of their sand bags to make it over the final hill.

definitions.definition: To let go or get rid of as being useless or defective.
To diffuse by osmosis.
To cause to diffuse by osmosis.
The net movement of solvent molecules, usually water, from a region of lower solute concentration to a region of higher solute concentration through a partially permeable membrane.
Picking up knowledge accidentally, without actually seeking that particular knowledge.
definitions.example: I was reading about chickens, and I guess I learned about hawks through osmosis.

definitions.definition: To discover or find by careful search, examination, or probing
Having been noticed.
definitions.example: The detected submarine was tracked by sonar, it was easy once they knew where to look.

definitions.definition: To make legally invalid.
definitions.example: The contract has been nullified.

definitions.definition: To prevent from happening.
To make of no use or value; to cancel out.
An auditory tone of low pitch or volume.

An implicit message perceived subtly alongside, but not detracting noticeably from, the explicit message conveyed in or by a book, film, verbal dialogue or similar (contrast with overtone); an undercurrent.
A pale colour, or one seen underneath another colour.
A low state of the physical faculties.
To accompany as an undertone.
To say or speak in an undertone.
To present as less important, noticeable or prominent.
A higher-pitched or stronger articulation of a particular syllable of a word or phrase in order to distinguish it from the others or to emphasize it.
definitions.example: In the word "careful", the accent is placed on the first syllable.

definitions.definition: Emphasis or importance in general.
definitions.example: At this hotel, the accent is on luxury.

definitions.definition: A mark or character used in writing, in order to indicate the place of the spoken accent, or to indicate the nature or quality of the vowel marked.
definitions.example: The name Cézanne is written with an acute accent.

definitions.definition: Modulation of the voice in speaking; the manner of speaking or pronouncing; a peculiar or characteristic modification of the voice, expressing emotion; tone.

The distinctive manner of pronouncing a language associated with a particular region, social group, etc., whether of a native speaker or a foreign speaker; the phonetic and phonological aspects of a dialect.
definitions.example: a broad Irish accent

definitions.definition: (sign languages) A distinctive manner of producing a sign language, such as someone who does not normally use a certain sign language might have when using it.
definitions.example: 2015 December 3, [./http//www.pri.org/stories/2015-12-03/philadelphia-accent-sign-language There's a distinctly Philadelphia accent in American Sign Language]

definitions.definition: A word; a significant tone or sound.
(usually plurale tantum) Expressions in general; speech.
Stress laid on certain syllables of a verse.
A regularly recurring stress upon the tone to mark the beginning, and, more feebly, the third part of the measure.
A special emphasis of a tone, even in the weaker part of the measure.
The rhythmical accent, which marks phrases and sections of a period.
The expressive emphasis and shading of a passage.
A mark used to represent specific stress on a note.
A mark placed at the right hand of a letter, and a little above it, to distinguish magnitudes of a similar kind expressed by the same letter, but differing in value, as y', y''.
A mark at the right hand of a number, indicating minutes of a degree, seconds, etc., as in 12' 27'', meaning twelve minutes and twenty-seven seconds.
A mark used to denote feet and inches, as in 6' 10'', meaning six feet ten inches.
Emphasis laid on a part of an artistic design or composition; an emphasized detail, in particular a detail in sharp contrast to its surroundings.
A very small gemstone set into a piece of jewellery.
A distinctive feature or quality.
Utterance.
One of the female (feminine) sex or gender.
Belonging to the sex which typically produces eggs (ova), or to the gender which is typically associated with it.
definitions.example: female authors, the leading male and female artists, a female bird cooing at a male, intersex female patients, a trans female vlogger

definitions.definition: Characteristic of this sex/gender. (Compare feminine, womanly.)
definitions.example: stereotypically female pastimes, an insect with typically female coloration

definitions.definition: Tending to lead to or regulate the development of sexual characteristics typical of this sex.
definitions.example: the female chromosome;   estrogen, the primary female sex hormone, is produced by both females and males

definitions.definition: (grammar, less common than 'feminine') Feminine; of the feminine grammatical gender.
Having an internal socket, as in a connector or pipe fitting.
To subdue someone by superior force.
definitions.example: We overpowered the opposing army within a couple of hours.

definitions.definition: To excel or exceed in power; to cause to yield; to subdue.
definitions.example: Bright light overpowers the eyes.

definitions.definition: To render imperceptible by means of greater strength, intensity etc.
definitions.example: The dish was OK, but the garlic slightly overpowered the herbs.

definitions.definition: To make excessively powerful.
Overly powerful.
The quality of being delicate.
Something appealing, usually a pleasing food, especially a choice dish of a certain culture suggesting rarity and refinement -a Chinese delicacy
Fineness or elegance of construction or appearance.
Frailty of health or fitness.
Refinement in taste or discrimination.
Tact and propriety; the need for such tact.
Containing nuts.
definitions.example: This is a nutty chocolate bar.

definitions.definition: Resembling or characteristic of nuts.
Barmy, crazy, mad.
definitions.example: Your wife is as nutty as a fruitcake.

definitions.definition: Extravagantly fashionable
(Australia and sometimes United States) A road junction at which traffic streams circularly around a central island.
A horizontal wheel which rotates around a central axis when pushed and on which children ride, often found in parks as a children's play apparatus.
A fairground carousel.
A detour.
A short, close-fitting coat or jacket worn by men or boys, especially in the 19th century.
A round dance.
Indirect, circuitous, or circumlocutionary.
Encircling; enveloping; comprehensive.
Of or relating to fable, myth or legend.
Characteristic of fables; marvelous, extraordinary, incredible.
Fictional or not believable; made up.
Known for telling fables or falsehoods; unreliable.
Very good; outstanding, wonderful.
Gay or pertaining to gay people.
Camp, effeminate.
Fashionable, glamorous
Tending to excite wonder; surprising, extraordinary.
Surprisingly excellent; very good or admirable, extremely impressive.
definitions.example: They served a wonderful six-course meal.

definitions.definition: Exceedingly, to a great extent.
A keepsake; an object kept as a reminder of a place or event.
definitions.example: I kept the shell as a memento of my visit to the seashore.

definitions.definition: Full extent; distance between extreme limits; especially; the distance between the nearest surfaces of two bodies, or the space between walls.
definitions.example: a room ten feet square in the clear

definitions.definition: The completion of a stage or challenge, or of the whole game.
A person who is free from the influence of engrams.
To remove obstructions, impediments or other unwanted items from.
definitions.example: If you clear the table, I'll wash up.

definitions.definition: To remove (items or material) so as to leave something unobstructed or open.
definitions.example: Please clear all this stuff off the table.

definitions.definition: To become free from obstruction or obscurement; to become transparent.
definitions.example: After a heavy rain, the sky cleared nicely for the evening.

definitions.definition: To eliminate ambiguity or doubt from (a matter); to clarify or resolve; to clear up.
definitions.example: We need to clear this issue once and for all.

definitions.definition: To remove from suspicion, especially of having committed a crime.
definitions.example: The court cleared the man of murder.

definitions.definition: To pass without interference; to miss.
definitions.example: The door just barely clears the table as it closes.

definitions.definition: (activities such as jumping or throwing) To exceed a stated mark.
definitions.example: She was the first female high jumper to clear two metres.

definitions.definition: To finish or complete (a stage, challenge, or game).
definitions.example: I cleared the first level in 36 seconds.

definitions.definition: Of a check or financial transaction, to go through as payment; to be processed so that the money is transferred.
definitions.example: The check might not clear for a couple of days.

definitions.definition: To earn a profit of; to net.
definitions.example: He's been clearing seven thousand a week.

definitions.definition: To approve or authorise for a particular purpose or action; to give clearance to.
definitions.example: Air traffic control cleared the plane to land.

definitions.definition: To obtain approval or authorisation in respect of.
definitions.example: I've cleared the press release with the marketing department, so go ahead and publish it.

definitions.definition: To obtain a clearance.
definitions.example: The steamer cleared for Liverpool today.

definitions.definition: To obtain permission to use (a sample of copyrighted audio) in another track.
To disengage oneself from incumbrances, distress, or entanglements; to become free.
To hit, kick, head, punch etc. (a ball, puck) away in order to defend one's goal.
definitions.example: A low cross came in, and Smith cleared.

definitions.definition: To reset or unset; to return to an empty state or to zero.
definitions.example: to clear an array;  to clear a single bit (binary digit) in a value

definitions.definition: To style (an element within a document) so that it is not permitted to float at a given position.
Transparent in colour.
definitions.example: as clear as crystal

definitions.definition: Bright, not dark or obscured.
definitions.example: Congress passed the President’s Clear Skies legislation.

definitions.definition: Free of obstacles.
definitions.example: The coast is clear.

definitions.definition: Without clouds.
definitions.example: clear weather; a clear day

definitions.definition: Of the sky, such that less than one eighth of its area is obscured by clouds.
Free of ambiguity or doubt.
definitions.example: Do I make myself clear? Crystal clear.

definitions.definition: Distinct, sharp, well-marked.
Free of guilt, or suspicion.
definitions.example: a clear conscience

definitions.definition: (of a soup) Without a thickening ingredient.
Possessing little or no perceptible stimulus.
definitions.example: clear of texture;  clear of odor

definitions.definition: Free from the influence of engrams; see Clear (Scientology).
Able to perceive clearly; keen; acute; penetrating; discriminating.
definitions.example: a clear intellect; a clear head

definitions.definition: Not clouded with passion; serene; cheerful.
Easily or distinctly heard; audible.
Unmixed; entirely pure.
definitions.example: clear sand

definitions.definition: Without defects or blemishes, such as freckles or knots.
definitions.example: a clear complexion; clear lumber

definitions.definition: Without diminution; in full; net.
definitions.example: a clear profit

definitions.definition: All the way; entirely.
definitions.example: I threw it clear across the river to the other side.

definitions.definition: Not near something or touching it.
definitions.example: Stand clear of the rails, a train is coming.

definitions.definition: Free (or separate) from others
In a clear manner; plainly.
A soft, fatty foodstuff made by churning the cream of milk (generally cow's milk).
Any of various foodstuffs made from other foods or oils, similar in consistency to, eaten like or intended as a substitute for butter (preceded by the name of the food used to make it).
definitions.example: peanut butter

definitions.definition: Any specific soft substance.
definitions.example: butter of antimony; butter of arsenic

definitions.definition: A smooth plane landing.
To spread butter on.
definitions.example: Butter the toast.

definitions.definition: To move one's weight backwards or forwards onto the tips or tails of one's skis or snowboard so only the tip or tail is in contact with the snow.
To increase (stakes) at every throw of dice, or every game.
The state of being bored.
An instance or period of being bored; A bored state.
(NNSE) The process of planning.
View, natural features, landscape.
Stage backdrops, property and other items on a stage that give the impression of the location of the scene.
The territory of a town.
A subdivision of a county.
(Pre 1994) An area set aside for nonwhite occupation.
(Post 1994) A nonwhite (usually subeconomic) area attached to a city.
A small town.

A disorder of the immune system causing adverse reactions to substances (allergens) not harmful to most and marked by the body's production of histamines and associated with atopy, anaphylaxis, and asthma.
Any condition of hypersensitivity to a substance.
Altered susceptibility to a first treatment as exhibited in reaction to a subsequent one.
An antipathy, as toward a person or activity.
A person who is transformed or can transform into a wolf or a wolflike human, often said to transform during a full moon.
People who are wicked.
Evil or mischievous by nature.
Excellent; awesome; masterful.
definitions.example: That was a wicked guitar solo, bro!

definitions.definition: Very, extremely.
definitions.example: The band we went to see the other night was wicked loud!

definitions.definition: A food made from ground roasted cocoa beans.
definitions.example: Chocolate is a very popular treat.

definitions.definition: A drink made by dissolving this food in boiling milk or water.
A single, small piece of confectionery made from chocolate.
definitions.example: He bought her some chocolates as a gift. She ate one chocolate and threw the rest away.

definitions.definition: A dark, reddish-brown colour/color, like that of chocolate (also called chocolate brown).
definitions.example: As he cooked it the whole thing turned a rich, deep chocolate.

definitions.definition: A black person; blackness.
(chiefly in the past participle) To add chocolate to; to cover (food) in chocolate.
To treat blood agar by heating in order to lyse the red blood cells in the medium.
Made of or containing chocolate.
Having a dark reddish-brown colour/color.
Black (relating to any of various ethnic groups having dark pigmentation of the skin).
An intense distressing emotion of fear or repugnance.
Something horrible; that which excites horror.
definitions.example: I saw many horrors during the war.

definitions.definition: Intense dislike or aversion; an abhorrence.
A genre of fiction designed to evoke a feeling of fear and suspense.
An individual work in this genre.
A nasty or ill-behaved person; a rascal or terror.
definitions.example: The neighbour's kids are a pack of little horrors!

definitions.definition: An intense anxiety or a nervous depression; often the horrors.
(plural) Delirium tremens.
The private part of an Arab household, traditionally forbidden to male strangers.
A group of someone's girlfriends, wives and/or concubines in a polygamous household.

A group of female animals (cows) herded and controlled by a male animal (bull) of that species for breeding purposes. Such behaviour is exhibited by bovids including cattle and buffalo as well as moose, elephants, seals, sea lions, baboons, and elephant seals.
Any significant number of women together as a group; a bevy.
A genre of anime and manga in which a man is the love interest of three or more women.
A temporary suspension of operation, especially of electrical power supply.
The amount of something lost in storage or transportation.
Freedom from some business or activity.
Free time given over to a specific purpose; occupation, activity.
A period during which official activity or business is formally suspended; an official holiday from university, law courts etc.
A holiday; a stretch of leisure time away from work or duty and devoted to rest or pleasure.
The act of vacating something; moving out.
definitions.example: The Conservative Party’s vacation of the centre ground gave an opportunity to its opponents.

definitions.definition: The act of making legally void.
To spend or take a vacation.
definitions.example: This year, we’re vacationing in Mexico.

definitions.definition: A violent flow, as of water, lava, etc.; a stream suddenly raised and running rapidly, as down a precipice.
definitions.example: A torrent of green and white water broke over the hull of the sail-boat.

definitions.definition: A large amount or stream of something.
definitions.example: They endured a torrent of inquiries.

definitions.definition: Rolling or rushing in a rapid stream.
A wet nurse.
A person (usually a woman) who takes care of other people’s young.
definitions.example: They hired a nurse to care for their young boy.

definitions.definition: A person trained to provide care for the sick.
definitions.example: The nurse made her rounds through the hospital ward.

definitions.definition: One who, or that which, brings up, rears, causes to grow, trains, or fosters.
definitions.example: Eton College has been called "the chief nurse of England's statesmen".

definitions.definition: A shrub or tree that protects a young plant.
A lieutenant or first officer who takes command when the captain is unfit for his place.
A larva of certain trematodes, which produces cercariae by asexual reproduction.
A nurse shark.
To breastfeed: to feed (a baby) at the breast; to suckle.
definitions.example: She believes that nursing her baby will make him strong and healthy.

definitions.definition: To breastfeed: to be fed at the breast.
To care for (someone), especially in sickness; to tend to.
definitions.example: She nursed him back to health.

definitions.definition: To treat kindly and with extra care
definitions.example: She nursed the rosebush and that season it bloomed.

definitions.definition: To manage with care and economy
To drink slowly
To foster, to nourish
To hold closely to one's chest
definitions.example: Would you like to nurse the puppy?

definitions.definition: To strike (billiard balls) gently, so as to keep them in good position during a series of shots.
(heading) A symbol or annotation.
(heading) A written or printed communication or commitment.
(heading) A sound.
Observation; notice; heed.
Reputation; distinction.
definitions.example: a poet of note

definitions.definition: Notification; information; intelligence.
Mark of disgrace.
To notice with care; to observe; to remark; to heed.
definitions.example: If you look to the left, you can note the old cathedral.

definitions.definition: To record in writing; to make a memorandum of.
definitions.example: We noted his speech.

definitions.definition: To denote; to designate.
definitions.example: The modular multiplicative inverse of x may be noted x-1.

definitions.definition: To annotate.
To set down in musical characters.
To record on the back of (a bill, draft, etc.) a refusal of acceptance, as the ground of a protest, which is done officially by a notary.
To take upon oneself; to start, to embark on (a specific task etc.).
To commit oneself (to an obligation, activity etc.).
definitions.example: He undertook to exercise more in future.

definitions.definition: To overtake on the wrong side.
definitions.example: I hate people who try and undertake on the motorway.

definitions.definition: To pledge; to assert, assure; to dare say.
To take by trickery; to trap, to seize upon.
To assume, as a character; to take on.
To engage with; to attack, take on in a fight.
To have knowledge of; to hear.
To have or take charge of.
An electrical engineer.
Related to electricity (or electronics)
A shrub of the genus Rosa, with red, pink, white or yellow flowers.
A flower of the rose plant.
A plant or species in the rose family. (Rosaceae)
Something resembling a rose flower.
The rose flower, usually depicted with five petals, five barbs, and a circular seed.
A purplish-red or pink colour, the colour of some rose flowers.
A round nozzle for a sprinkling can or hose.
The base of a light socket.
Any of various large, red-bodied, papilionid butterflies of the genus Pachliopta.
Any of various flower-like polar graphs of sinusoids or their squares.
A graph with only one vertex.
To make rose-coloured; to redden or flush.
To perfume, as with roses.
A pale pink wine made by removing the dark grape skins at the required point during fermentation.
A kind of aquatic plant, genus Nelumbo in the family Nelumbonaceae.
A water lily, genus Nymphaea, especially those of Egypt or India.
A legendary plant eaten by the Lotophagi of the Odyssey that caused drowsiness and euphoria.
A number of other plants bearing lotus in their scientific or common names (see Derived terms below).
An architectural motif of ancient Egyptian temples.
The planet Venus as observed in the eastern sky around dawn.
definitions.example: The evening star is the morning star. (Hesperus is Phosphorus.)

definitions.definition: Lucifer, Satan, the devil.
(less commonly) The planet Mercury as observed in the eastern sky around dawn.
A weapon consisting of a heavy ball set with spikes attached rigidly to a staff, in contrast to a flail.
One who is expert at programming and solving problems with a computer.
One who uses a computer to gain unauthorized access to data, or to carry out malicious attacks.
A computer security professional.
Something that hacks; a tool or device for hacking.
A fork-shaped tool used to harvest root vegetables.
Someone who hacks.
One who is inexperienced or unskilled at a particular activity, especially a sport such as golf or tennis.
definitions.example: a tennis hacker

definitions.definition: One who operates a taxicab
To occur or take place.
definitions.example: Let me tell you how it happened.

definitions.definition: To happen to; to befall.
(with infinitive) To do or occur by chance or unexpectedly.
definitions.example: Do you happen to have an umbrella?

definitions.definition: (followed by on or upon) To encounter by chance.
Maybe, perhaps.
(of a person or place) Very beautiful.
definitions.example: All the contest judges agreed that Brigitt was absolutely gorgeous.

definitions.definition: Very enjoyable, pleasant, tasty, etc.
definitions.example: Hummus is absolutely gorgeous.

definitions.definition: A thing whose name cannot be remembered; thingamajig, doohickey.
Any device or machine, especially one whose name cannot be recalled. Often either clever or complicated.
definitions.example: He bought a neat new gadget for shredding potatoes.

definitions.definition: Any consumer electronics product.
A sequence of machine code instructions crafted as part of an exploit that attempts to divert execution to a memory location chosen by the attacker.
A piece of equipment that may be strapped to a person’s back and generate vertical thrust by a downward discharge of jet fluid, thereby rendering the person capable of flight.
To travel with a jet pack.
definitions.example: the first person to jetpack around the world

definitions.definition: Discarded or waste material; rubbish, trash.
A collection of miscellaneous items of little value.
Any narcotic drug, especially heroin.
The genitalia.
Salt beef.
Pieces of old cable or cordage, used for making gaskets, mats, swabs, etc., and when picked to pieces, forming oakum for filling the seams of ships.
A fragment of any solid substance; a thick piece; a chunk.
Material or resources of a kind lacking commercial value.
definitions.example: junk fish; junk trees

definitions.definition: To throw away.
To find something for very little money (meaning derived from the term junk shop)
definitions.example: (On Facebook, a record collector wrote:) "The newest addition to my Annette Hanshaw collection, I junked this beautiful flawless E-copy within walking distance from my house."

definitions.definition: The internal workings of a mechanism or system that are normally hidden from view
The loud rumbling, cracking, or crashing sound caused by expansion of rapidly heated air around a lightning bolt.
definitions.example: Thunder is preceded by lightning.

definitions.definition: A deep, rumbling noise resembling thunder.
definitions.example: Off in the distance, he heard the thunder of hoofbeats, signalling a stampede.

definitions.definition: An alarming or startling threat or denunciation.
The discharge of electricity; a thunderbolt.
The spotlight.
definitions.example: Shortly after I announced my pregnancy, he stole my thunder with his news of landing his dream job.

definitions.definition: To produce thunder; to sound, rattle, or roar, as a discharge of atmospheric electricity; often used impersonally.
definitions.example: It thundered continuously.

definitions.definition: To make a noise like thunder.
definitions.example: The train thundered along the tracks.

definitions.definition: To talk with a loud, threatening voice.
To say (something) with a loud, threatening voice.
definitions.example: "Get back to work at once!", he thundered.

definitions.definition: To produce something with incredible power
One who has done something offensive; a deceitful, dishonest, or disreputable person; a deceiver; a cheat.
A rogue; rascal; miscreant; a ne'er-do-well.
Dishonest; resulting from or inspired by dishonesty.
(somewhat derogatory) A person who is habitually drunk.
To fix (an object) to another object using a nail.
definitions.example: He nailed the placard to the post.

definitions.definition: To drive a nail.
definitions.example: He used the ax head for nailing.

definitions.definition: To stud or boss with nails, or as if with nails.
To catch.
To expose as a sham.
To accomplish (a task) completely and successfully.
definitions.example: I really nailed that test.

definitions.definition: To hit (a target) effectively with some weapon.
Of a male, to engage in sexual intercourse with.
To spike, as a cannon.
To nail down: to make certain, or confirm.
Having nails (often of a specified kind).
definitions.example: a red-nailed finger

definitions.definition: Effective management of a community or system, or especially its resources.
The study of money, currency and trade, and the efficient use of resources.
Frugal use of resources.
definitions.example: economy of word

definitions.definition: The system of production and distribution and consumption. The overall measure of a currency system; as the national economy.
The method of divine government of the world. (See w:Economy (religion).)
The part of a commercial passenger airplane or train reserved for those paying the lower standard fares; economy class.
Management of one’s residency.
Cheap to run; using minimal resources; representing good value for money.
definitions.example: Economy size.

definitions.definition: Hell; the bottomless pit; primeval chaos; a confined subterranean ocean.
(frequently figurative) A bottomless or unfathomed depth, gulf, or chasm; hence, any deep, immeasurable; any void space.
Anything infinite, immeasurable, or profound.
Moral depravity; vast intellectual or moral depth.
An impending catastrophic happening.
The center of an escutcheon.
The abyssal zone.
A difference, especially a large difference, between groups.
The inedible parts of a grain-producing plant.
definitions.example: To separate out the chaff, early cultures tossed baskets of grain into the air and let the wind blow away the lighter chaff.

definitions.definition: Straw or hay cut up fine for the food of cattle.
Any excess or unwanted material, resource, or person; anything worthless.
Light jesting talk; banter; raillery.
Loose material, e.g. small strips of aluminum foil dropped from aircraft, intended to interfere with radar detection.
An opening, usually covered by one or more panes of clear glass, to allow light and air from outside to enter a building or vehicle.
An opening, usually covered by glass, in a shop which allows people to view the shop and its products from outside; a shop window.
The shutter, casement, sash with its fittings, or other framework, which closes a window opening.
A period of time when something is available.
definitions.example: launch window;  window of opportunity;  You have a two-hour window of clear weather to finish working on the lawn.

definitions.definition: A restricted range.

A rectangular area on a computer terminal or screen containing some kind of user interface, displaying the output of and allowing input for one of a number of simultaneously running computer processes.
A figure formed of lines crossing each other.
The time between first infection and detectability.
To furnish with windows.
To place at or in a window.
To act or behave in an inappropriate, improper, incorrect, or unexpected manner.
definitions.example: He doesn't mean to misbehave; he just doesn't know better.

definitions.definition: An insect in the order Orthoptera, especially family Gryllidae, that makes a chirping sound by rubbing its wing casings against combs on its hind legs.
A wooden footstool.
A signalling device used by soldiers in hostile territory to identify themselves to a friendly in low visibility conditions.
A relatively small area of a roof constructed to divert water from a horizontal intersection of the roof with a chimney, wall, expansion joint or other projection.
A fixture attached to a wall to hold up a shelf.
Any intermediate object that connects a smaller part to a larger part, the smaller part typically projecting sideways from the larger part.
A short crooked timber, resembling a knee, used as a support.
The cheek or side of an ordnance carriage, supporting the trunnions.
Any of the characters "(", ")", "[", "]", "{", "}", "<" and ">", used in pairs to enclose parenthetic remarks, sections of mathematical expressions, etc.
A printed diagram of games in a tournament.
A prediction of the outcome of games in a tournament, used for betting purposes.
One of several ranges of numbers.
definitions.example: tax bracket, age bracket

definitions.definition: A pair of values that represent the smallest and largest elements of a range.
In artillery, the endangered region between two shell impacts (one long and one short). The next shell fired is likely to hit accurately.
The small curved or angular corner formed by a serif and a stroke in a letter.
(land surveying, 19th century) a mark cut into a stone by land surveyors to secure a bench.
To support by means of mechanical brackets.
To enclose in typographical brackets.
To bound on both sides, to surround, as enclosing with brackets.
definitions.example: I tried to hit the bullseye by first bracketing it with two shots and then splitting the difference with my third, but I missed.

definitions.definition: To place in the same category.
definitions.example: Because the didn't have enough young boys for two full teams, they bracketed the seven-year olds with the eight-year olds.

definitions.definition: To mark distinctly for special treatment.
To set aside, discount, ignore.
To take multiple images of the same subject, using a range of exposure settings, in order to help ensure that a satisfactory image is obtained.

(phenomenology) In the philosophical system of Edmund Husserl and his followers, to set aside metaphysical theories and existential questions concerning what is real in order to focus philosophical attention simply on the actual content of experience.
The hard white form of dentin which forms the tusks of elephants, walruses and other animals.
A creamy white color, the color of ivory.
Something made from or resembling ivory.
(collective, singular or in plural) The teeth.
(collective, singular or in plural) The keys of a piano.
A white person.
Made of ivory.
Resembling or having the colour of ivory.
To break or disregard (a rule or convention).
definitions.example: Accessing unauthorized files violates security protocol.

definitions.definition: To rape.
To cite (a person) for a parole violation.
A type of small boat, covered over by a surface deck, powered by the occupant or occupants using a double-bladed paddle in a sitting position, from a hole in the surface deck
To use a kayak, to travel or race in a kayak.
definitions.example: Kayaking is an Olympic sport.

definitions.definition: To traverse (a body of water) by kayak.
definitions.example: On a dare, he kayaked the Harlem River in New York from Hell's Gate to Spyten Duyvil.

definitions.definition: A go-cart.
A young goat.
definitions.example: He treated the oxen like they didn't exist, but he treated the goat kid like a puppy.

definitions.definition: Of a female goat, the state of being pregnant: in kid.
Kidskin.
The meat of a young goat.
A young antelope.
A child (usually), teenager, or young adult; a juvenile.
definitions.example: She's a kid. It's normal for her to have imaginary friends.

definitions.definition: A person whose childhood took place in a particular time period or area.
definitions.example: He's been living in Los Angeles for years now, but he's a Florida kid.

definitions.definition: One's son or daughter, regardless of age.
definitions.example: He was their youngest kid.

definitions.definition: (in the vocative) Used as a form of address for a child, teenager or young adult
definitions.example: No, kid, you didn't do anything wrong; they did!

definitions.definition: An inexperienced person or one in a junior position.
A deception; an act of kidding somebody.
A small wooden mess tub in which sailors received their food.
To make a fool of (someone).
To dupe or deceive (someone).
To make a joke with (someone).
Of a goat, to give birth to kids.
To joke.
definitions.example: Only kidding

definitions.definition: A unique example or specimen of something
The appearance of Jesus Christ to the Magi on the twelfth day after Christmas.
An annual Christian feast celebrating this event.
The day of the celebration, January 6th, or sometimes (in Western Christianity), the Sunday between January 2nd and 8th.

The season or time of the Christian church year either from the Epiphany feast day to Shrove Tuesday (the day before Ash Wednesday, the start of Lent) or from the Epiphany feast day to the feast of the Presentation of Christ in the Temple.
An illuminating realization or discovery, often resulting in a personal feeling of elation, awe, or wonder.
definitions.example: It came to her in an epiphany what her life's work was to be.

definitions.definition: A manifestation or appearance of a divine or superhuman being.
Any beetle of the family Lampyridae, which exhibit bioluminescence during twilight.
To sputter or hiss.
definitions.example: The soda fizzled for several minutes after it was poured.

definitions.definition: To decay or die off to nothing; to burn out; to end less successfully than previously hoped.
definitions.example: The entire project fizzled after the founder quit.
